rulings.Case Title: FEI SU ET AL VS. HUI QIN WU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For A Judgment On The Pleadings; Request For Judical Notice In Support Of Motion For A Judgment On The Pleadings; And Declaration Of Curtis E. Smolar In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 10, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT HUI WU, WEI TAN, SIMON TAN NOTICE OF DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; REQUEST FOR JUDICAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS; AND DECLARATION OF CURTIS E. SMOLAR IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the 2nd Cause of Action for violation of SFRO 37.10B for Plaintiff to allege any violations within 1 year of the filing of the complaint. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the 5th cause of action for violation of SFRO 37.11A to allege (1) whether P is alleging a violation solely based on retaliation and is NOT seeking treble damages, compare paragraphs 181 and 188; (2) if plaintiff is seeking treble damages, alleging applicable violations within 1 year of the filing of the complaint; and (3) if Plaintiff is solely alleging violations based on retaliation, allege retaliatory acts within 3 years of the filing of the complaint. Motion is otherwise DENIED. Unlike in Manefee, the remaining causes of action do not arise out of the violation of SFRO 37.10B or 37.11A, while they may be supported by some of the same facts that support those causes of action. See Menefee v. Ostawari (1991) 228 Cal.App.3d 239 =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SPALDING VS. VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 10, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT EMERALD FUND INC., EMERALD VAN NESS HAYES LLC, MARC BABSIN, 150 VAN NESS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, VAN NESS HAYES ASSOCIATES, LLC, SILVANUS ERICKSON DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with a final leave to amend. Plaintiff has been previously ordered to allege her claims in clear and concise language. Plaintiff has not done so. Plaintiff has been previously ordered to make factual allegations in support of each cause of action as to each defendant. Plaintiff has not done so. Instead, the Complaint appears to be a compilation of citations to various laws, cases, and secondary sources combined with discussions of discovery motions in this case, and general allegations of wrongdoing by all defendants without little to no differentiation. This complaint is impossible to follow or respond to. Plaintiff shall have 60 days to file an amended complaint. Plaintiff shall allege facts (1) in support of each element or (2) each cause of action; (3) as to each defendant. By way of an example, Plaintiff purports to assert a cause of action for breach of contract as to 11 listed defendants. As to each of these Defendants the Plaintiff must allege: (1) a contract and attach it, e.g. contract with Emerald Fund Inc; contract with Marc Babsin; contract with National Real Estate Development LLC, etc.); (2) facts showing that Plaintiff performed under each of the alleged contracts or is excused from performance; (3) each defendant breached their respective contract with the Plaintiff; and (4) resulting damages to the Plaintiff from that defendant's breach of that particular contract. Plaintiff shall refrain from citing cases (e.g. page 154 lines 12-19; page 160, lines 21-25), discussing legal concepts that are irrelevant to the particular cause of action (e.g. Intentional, knowing and fraudulent misrepresentation at page 154 line 16; concealment at page 155, line 22; strict liability of manufacturers at page 157, line 11-24; foreseeability, page 164, starting at line 11 ) and facts that have no apparent connection to the particular causes of action (see page 148, lines 15-17). The Court notes that the operative complaint is excessively long, repetitive, includes lengthy discussions of law and legal concepts, and does not tie whatever facts are being alleged to particular causes of action and particular defendants. Plaintiff shall attempt to make the complaint shorter, not longer. Plaintiff is reminded that complaint must be alleged in clear and concise language and must contain ultimate facts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 10, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT MATTHEW PROTACIO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each of his causes of action. The Court notes that the first cause of action for harassment appears to be based solely on CC 527.6. If that is what Plaintiff attempts to allege, this claim must be brought in department 505, not in a civil action. The Court further notes that this claim appears to be based solely on the events of October 2, 2023, and those events have already been presented to Judge Evangelista resulting in November 30, 2023 order as evidenced by the assertions in the Declaration attached to the Complaint as Exhibit 1. The Court further notes that there are only 3 causes of action listed in the Complaint, however, Plaintiff references 4 causes of action in opposition. Plaintiff must clearly label his causes of action in the amended complaint and include facts in support of each element of each labeled causes of action. Conclusory general statements (e.g. "On or about April 2024 Portacio in concert with his lawyer Marc Musante arranged to set up the plaintiff by filming him in his gallery; Complaint, paragraph 23) are not sufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
